The user wants a PDDL plan for a specific problem instance.
The problem is about rotating a string of nodes.
The domain defines a specific rotation mechanism involving three phases:
1. `rotate`: Initiates the rotation at a specific node `?n` with a rotation `?r`. It updates the heading of `?n` and sets up the first pass cursor.
2. `rotate-first-pass`: Iterates through the string starting from the node after `?n`. It updates the heading of the next node based on the rotation and clears its position (sets `free`). It moves the cursor to the next node.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`: Marks the end of the first pass when the end node is reached. It clears the end node's position and sets up the second pass cursor.
4. `rotate-second-pass`: Iterates through the string again. It sets the position (`at`) of the next node based on the new heading and the previous node's position. It moves the cursor.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`: Marks the end of the second pass when the end node is reached. It clears the second pass cursor and removes the `rotating` flag.
